TLN.inp.published_best_source: Siew, C., & Tanyimboh, T. T. (2012). Penalty-Free Feasibility Boundary Convergent Multi-Objective Evolutionary Algorithm for the Optimization of Water Distribution Systems. https://strathprints.strath.ac.uk/43222/1/Siew_Tanyimboh_PFMOEA.pdf
TLN.inp.constraints: minimum pressure head >= 30m above elevation at demand nodes
TLN.inp.simulation_assumptions: Hazen-Williams, typically C=130, EPANET-style hydraulic feasibility
TLN.inp.units: USD for cost, meters for lengths/heads
TLN.inp.notes: Best value 419000 confirmed in Siew & Tanyimboh (2012), Section 5.1/Table 1; Exeter link retained for benchmark data/spec context.

NYT.inp.published_best_source: Murphy, Simpson & Dandy (1993/1994); benchmark summaries and later optimization studies citing best feasible NYT solution ≈ $38.642M.
NYT.inp.cost_definition: nonlinear pipe duplication cost function from NYT benchmark literature
NYT.inp.constraints: minimum pressure head >= 255 ft at all nodes except node 16 >= 260 ft, node 17 >= 272.8 ft, source node 1 = 300 ft
NYT.inp.simulation_assumptions: Hazen-Williams, typically C=100, EPANET-style hydraulic feasibility
NYT.inp.units: USD for cost, feet in original benchmark (network often converted to meters)
NYT.inp.notes: NYT literature contains multiple reported minima depending on HW constants; 38.642M is a commonly cited feasible benchmark under standard settings.

GOY.inp.published_best_source: Optimization of Water Distribution Networks Using Genetic Algorithm Based SOP-WDN Program (Water 2022), plus benchmark reviews citing best feasible GoYang cost.
GOY.inp.constraints: minimum pressure head >= 15m above elevation at all demand nodes
GOY.inp.simulation_assumptions: Hazen-Williams, C=100, single fixed pump 4.52 kW, EPANET hydraulic feasibility
GOY.inp.units: KRW (Won) for cost, meters for lengths/heads
GOY.inp.notes: Benchmark has 30 pipes, 22 nodes, 9 loops, 1 reservoir/tank and fixed pump. Best feasible cost commonly reported as 177,010,359 Won.

HAN.inp.published_best_source: Secondary citation from review/summary material: Lim, S. H., Lee, C. W., Hong, S. J., & Yoo, D. G. (2020), Journal of the Korea Academia-Industrial cooperation Society, 21(5), 295-303; and user-provided benchmark summary (H527) reporting Hanoi global optimum = $6.081M.
HAN.inp.source_reliability: Medium (derived from secondary summary sources; primary Fujiwara & Khang table/page still recommended)
HAN.inp.constraints: minimum surplus head >= 30m at demand nodes
HAN.inp.simulation_assumptions: Hazen-Williams, commonly C=130, EPANET-style hydraulic feasibility
HAN.inp.units: USD for cost, meters for lengths/heads
HAN.inp.notes: Method (34 pipes, 32 nodes, 3 loops, diameter set, unit costs, min head 30m) verified. Best value 6.081M is currently supported by secondary sources; add primary Fujiwara & Khang page/table citation when available.

BIN.inp.published_best_source: Secondary citation from BN case-study reporting best solution = €1.956M and attributing cost basis to Reca & Martínez (2006).
BIN.inp.source_reliability: Medium (best value and method verified from secondary summaries/images; primary Reca & Martínez table/appendix still recommended)
BIN.inp.constraints: minimum pressure head >= 20m at demand nodes
BIN.inp.simulation_assumptions: Darcy-Weisbach with roughness epsilon = 0.0025mm (Moody/Colebrook model), EPANET hydraulic feasibility
BIN.inp.units: EUR for cost, meters for lengths/heads
BIN.inp.notes: Method (454 pipes, 443 nodes, DW roughness 0.0025mm, min head 20m) verified via user-provided secondary sources. Keep €1.956M as current working reference; H527 summaries may round to €1.94M. Replace with primary Reca & Martínez citation once obtained.
